text.title: License Compliance Scan Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for gov-license-compliance-scan. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Scanner version MUST be pinned in CI (Docker tag or checksum); 'latest' is forbidden because scanner-rule drift changes verdicts silently.
rule.rationale: Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) practice — pinned scanner is the only way to make verdicts diff-reviewable.

rule.statement: Allowed SPDX ids MUST be enumerated in `compliance/licenses.yaml`; unknown licenses default to denied.
rule.rationale: SPDX license list — default-deny is the only safe policy for legal exposure.

rule.statement: Denylist MUST include AGPL-*, GPL-3.0*, GPL-2.0* (without classpath exception), SSPL-* by default; exceptions require written justification + legal sign-off.
rule.rationale: SFLC + Heather Meeker license guidance — strong copyleft in distributed product is the highest-risk class.

rule.statement: Scan MUST resolve transitive dependencies (not just direct); license risk lives in the deep graph.
rule.rationale: Snyk + GitHub dependency-graph guidance — direct-only scans miss the dominant risk surface.

rule.statement: PRs that introduce a denylisted SPDX id MUST be blocked; override requires `legal-approved` label + linked exception record.
rule.rationale: Compliance audit best practice — silent merges of GPL deps are the most cited audit findings.

rule.statement: Nightly scan MUST run on main and emit a tracking ticket on any new finding; transitive deps can shift without PR.
rule.rationale: Renovate / Dependabot drift patterns — transitive upgrades introduce new licenses outside PR scope.

rule.statement: Per-release SBOM (CycloneDX or SPDX) MUST be published alongside the artifact; downstream consumers must be able to verify.

rationale.source: EU CRA + US EO 14028 SBOM requirements

rule.rationale: EU CRA + US EO 14028 SBOM requirements — SBOM publication is becoming regulatory baseline.

rule.statement: If product has zero external distribution + low IP exposure, return `skipped`.
